channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: You faggots don't understand money. You think a few million will make you happy. My income puts me in the top 2% of the… — 19 replies — 76.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
You faggots don't understand money. You think a few million will make you happy. My income puts me in the top 2% of the…

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-04-23 03:28 UTC

OP

You faggots don't understand money. You think a few million will make you happy. My income puts me in the top 2% of the US and I feel like a literal pauper. After my student loans, taxes, mortgage, wife, and kids leech my paycheck, I have almost nothing left over. I can't even save. Count yourself lucky if you're a NEET with no responsibilities. You live in literal paradise compared to me.

Early replies (first 3)

62142491
>>62142485
mom said it's my turn to post low effort garbage as a consequence of being bullied/molested/ignored as a child

62142505
womp womp

62142517
>>62142485
I'm a single man living off VA benefis, I invest everything into btc. I imagine some dumb cunt and kids would make my life miserable but overall life is based

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62142528
>>62142485
Sounds like you're a wagie. Pretty pathetic.

62142586
>>62142485
That's your own fault for being bad at planning and managing your money.

62142595
>>62142485
thanks for your blogpost, fag

i saw a study in 2018 which measured subjective happiness & income level
at the time, i think around $60,000 per year per person correlated strongly with happiness, but any income above that amount didn't correlate much with happiness
it makes sense to me, minimum standards of quality of life etc. but beyond that has nothing to do with it
u gotta account for inflation if you wanna use the figure of $60,000 as a reference i guess

62142610
another interesting fact, suicide rates in a country actually correlate strongly with higher standards of living

if ur a dentist in some scandinavian country, maybe you have a lot of comfort and time to ponder things, and maybe ur more prone to feel like things are meaningless
if you're in dire straits or homeless or a lithium miner in africa, you don't have time to think too much, it's an entirely different state of consciousness altogether
worrying about the next day instead of the next 10 years, not focusing on yourself
it's interesting

62142611
>>62142485
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

62142685
Lmao just leave

62142705
>>62142485
I understand money.
4% rule puts me at around $3500/month I can spend.
I literally spend 6 months out of the year traveling, own my farm house with low property tax, very low cost of living.
Don't really have to work but i work sometimes as an IT Tech just for fun. I use that money for hobbies.
$1mil is more than enough desu.

62142725
>>62142705
nice blog retard
>>62142611
>>62142685
retard

this is supposed to be an autism website but it's just twitter now

62142790
>>62142485
if i had 2 million dollars right now, i could quit working, live better than i currently do, and it would grow to 5 million in like 10 years

62142905
>>62142485
>You faggots don't understand money.
>has loans and a mortgage he can't afford, wastes money on le vagina

62142910
An income doesn’t make you rich. You are poor unless your investments cover your basic needs

62142921
>>62142485
>top 2% income
>i don't gots no moneys sob sob sob
nigga i think you don't understand money

62143225
>>62142485
My wife makes 2x what I do. Better luck next time jew.

62143271
>>62142485
>fiat currency brain
Nigger you don't understand jack from shit

62145814
>>62143271
If you're so smart, what would you do differently?

62145826
>>62142610
Very true, said African lithium miner will throw a party if you throw him a dime, whereas a rich man will not celebrate a thousand bucks. Hedonic treadmill or something. Nonetheless, I'm pretty happy.

item.title: >nofap >religion >stock tips >meditation >therapy >healing crystals >self-help books >pomodoro >PDFs >affirmations >posi… — 37 replies — 67.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
>nofap >religion >stock tips >meditation >therapy >healing crystals >self-help books >pomodoro >PDFs >affirmations >posi…

Open thread • Replies: 37 • Updated: 2026-04-23 03:07 UTC

OP

>nofap
>religion
>stock tips
>meditation
>therapy
>healing crystals
>self-help books
>pomodoro
>PDFs
>affirmations
>positive thinking
>DCA
>crypto
>"self improvement"

Early replies (first 3)

62143060
>/biz/: Business & Finance

62143073
Different ways of coping. No boomber ever had to read the meditations or go to the gym

62143145
>>62143073
the boomers who did DCA have generational wealth now

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62144289
>>62144272
>only thing that matters in this life
You mean easiest way to cope with the meaningless existence

62144301
>>62144272
just as fentanyl

62144309
>>62144289
Exactly this. It works on most people, and why shouldn't it. The smart ones are the truly fucked. Because we have to live in a constant state of realization of the madness of it all, and most of all, the inescapability.

62144381
>>62144238
That's frankly a pretty retarded way of looking at it
Women have been unfaithful since the dawn of time and the only thing you can blame aurelius for was trusting somebody with bad faith actions. Getting assblasted because of every little thing is peak niggerdom and if rome acted like that (there are periods they did), the world would have been much worse off for it. Don't be le stoic and force yourself to passively take every loss, failure, and slight like a doormat; just don't chimp out at every opportunity because you think you can

62144395
>>62143054
All you need is Catholicism and the rest will work itself out.

62144435
>>62144381
>Women have been unfaithful since the dawn of time
>trusted a woman
Why he trusted a woman if you say women were always unfaithful dumbass?
Your statement is very incosistent, he is not to blame for trusting a woman yet you say trusting a woman is retarded. So why you defend him. Dumb. I really don't get the Marcus Aurelius dickride

62144457
>>62144435
That's not even what I said, but I'm not surprised you can't see past an argument and a mindset somebody else gave you. 'Women have been unfaithful for forever' is not the same as 'every woman is going to be unfaithful for forever'
I also dont follow stoicism, that macro's reasoning is retarded is my whole point. Defending against bad arguments is also not the same as 'dickriding,' but being brown will put you at a disadvantage for understanding that as well
>inb4 n-not brown! n-no u!
Then stop thinking like a nigger

62144475
>>62143054
>self affirmations are things people that made it do
I actually say
>i am top 10%
>i made it
Multiple times a day

62144478
>>62144457
>Women have been unfaithful for forever' is not the same as 'every woman is going to be unfaithful for forever
Good thing nobody wants advice about women from retards with such retarded statements

62144497
fcuk you OP I scrolled down and was accidentally breathing in when I saw that image and now I feel weird.

62144501
>>62144289
Only people who believe that their ancestors were shat out of a monkeys ass believe there is no meaning to this life.

62144519
>>62144501
Lol ok kid let's see if you gonna say that on your deathbed or if you get cancer. Retarded NPCs like you will never be saved

62144572
>>62144475
>top 10%
>made it

kek

62144576
>>62143145
a monk would live like this but without the faggot TV and gaming setup
that's what bothers me about these "Let me guess. You need more?" images, they're not based and ascetic if you have a faggot TV

62144982
>>62144519
Wtf is that even supposed to mean?

62144995
>>62144982
There's a few active anons on the board that just try to be as caustic and derisive towards any topic or poster as possible. There's at least 2 itt

62144996
>>62144982
It means you are a retarded brownoid faggot basedboy cuck. Kill yourself

62145001
>>62144995
>>62144996
Boom right on cue
They are generally pretty quick to reply as well because that kind of derangement generally stems from being terminally online

62145005
I've read
>how to win friends and influence people
>art of war
>12 rules by peterson
>48 laws of power
>atomic habits
>the art of not giving a fuck
>the art of the deal
>meditations of auruelius(stoic)
>4 hour work week
>the secret
>rich dad poor dad and the sequel

And yet my life still sucks. And before you say "but anon your'e supposed to do the things the books say to do not just read them!" well I have. For example richdad poordad says to borrow money from your friends and buy a house, flip it and then give them back their money-the exact amount they let you borrow. Well I asked all my friends for money and NONE of them gave me any. Only one even asked what it was for and when I told him he laughed in my face and said "You have to know how to flip a house to flip a house."

What the fuck

62145007
>>62145001
>>62144995
>generally pretty quick to reply
The irony. Also you have to be 18+ to post here

62145009
>>62144501
>the meaning to this life is uhhhh if you're a good boy and follow this book, you'll have an infinitely long life after this one, 100% guaranteed, and in that life everything will be great, okay?

62145415
>>62143054
>>nofap
Difficult to do, confers immediate health and psychological benefits, and every normie has a bizarre level hatred for it.

That should tell you something.

62145428
>>62144238
Of course stoicism is a cuck mentality. It's a philosophy of decadence. No Greek during the peak of their civilization would have created something like stoicism the same way no boomber would have been interested in the meditations by marcus aurelius.

When the real world becomes too painful, many people retire and create ways of coping.

62145464
>>62145428
Real stoicism is a an "emptying"/devotion of yourself to higher Being. This is a living, communicative path that taps you into energetic circuits and correspondence. Stoicism as most people know it isn't transformative in any higher sense.

62145495
>>62145464
Well, I meant decadence in the societal sense. Individuals live in a society. Stoicism was born exactly when the greek dream ended and that's why it became so popular. Same way as Epicureism.

And that's why it is so goddamn popular now and you have depressed 21 yo kids talking about stoicism. It has become a meme. You're depressed? Read The Meditations and go to the gym, bro. The real world has become painful and people have to find ways to deal with the real world.

ps. By the way, Seneca moral writings are way more interesting than Marcus Aurelius' Meditations

62145579
>>62145009
Correct
>t. full trust that I'm where I need to be when I need to be there

62145590
>>62145579
retardmaxxing with extra steps

62145626
>>62145590
I wish I could impart to you even 1% of the things I've seen, alas just know its sort of like a Chinese finger trap you know what I mean?
>retardmaxxing
Okay, okay! Based!

62145653
>>62143054
i went all in on long distance ejaculation training, sucks to suck, peace i gotta catch a flight, there are some ceilings that need coating

62145752
>>62145005
rich dad poor dad taught me to put all my money in pokemon cards

item.title: What the best ROI investment I can make on myself? — 14 replies — 56.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
What the best ROI investment I can make on myself?

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-04-23 03:35 UTC

OP

What the best ROI investment I can make on myself?

Early replies (first 3)

62145347
>>62145342
get a high paying job

62145354
>>62145342
500 test a week

62145355
>>62145347
By just being lucky?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62145359
>>62145342
Paging dr brosnan...

62145361
>>62145355
nevermind lmao

62145362
>>62145354
Huh?

62145364
>>62145342
HRT and a good tuck game

62145478
>>62145342
imagine she just got done taking a poo haha

62145490
>>62145342
a good wife who loves you

62145494
>>62145342
look at those long flat heavy saggers

62145540
>>62145342
i'd go all in on inventing a time machine if i could go back...its too late for me now but you can do it

62145596
>>62145342
Clean your toilet you ugly, stinky, vile bitch.

62145686
>>62145490
Rated
Under
>nightmare quest

62145864
>>62145342
Go back and choose a better Parents
lol jk
Get a better job and take care of your body and skin. The younger you start the better the results

item.title: Why do people kiss the job market's ass so badly? — 22 replies — 21.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why do people kiss the job market's ass so badly?

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-04-23 02:38 UTC

OP

> It's not them, IT'S YOU AND YOUR BAD RESUME BRO!
> you sent your resume to an expert already to get reviewed? YOU'RE APPLYING TO THE WRONG FIELDS BRO!
> no no no no you can't spam your resume to hundreds of locations, YOU HAVE THE TAILOR YOUR RESUME TO EACH AND EVERY JOB BRO
> not getting interviews? JUST BE A SIGMA CUM LAUDE FROM A TOP 10 UNIVERSITY BRO, JUST HAVE (CORRECT) EXPERIENCE BRO
> you got rejected after your interview? STOP BEING AN AUTIST BRO

You hear this shit literally everywhere. None of the above should ever be considered normal, but people have still normalized every job having literally 0.01% odds of you landing it with a pacific ocean sized competition pool for each of those jobs. Why do people insist that you're the problem and not the job market & corrupt HR & greedy cunt corp execs? Why do people love to kiss the "job market"'s ass so much?!

Early replies (first 3)

62138902
Applying to jobs is a numbers game. I had solid experience and two degrees, it took me a year to find another role. We’re basically in a recession with the jobs market and have been since 2023. Anyone saying you need to do this or that is coping, just keep applying and don’t be an autist during your interview.

62138903
Because if they admit the job market is ass they have to admit that they gamed the system via nepotism which indirectly tells them that they aren't as qualified as they think they are.

62138948
>>62138892
yeah its cringe same when they lick the billionaires ass

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62138969
>>62138892
Because of the
>blame yourself (not others)
Psyop the jews put out there to avoid blame. Therefore anything bad happening to you is your fault. And it's a very long running psyop.

62139019
>>62138892
they're literally retarded. these are the same people who will crucify you for victim blaming a woman who was raped after dressing like a whore.

62139088
>>62138892
the problem is the job market overall isn't bad, its specifically tech. People leave this key detail out of every debate about the job market. We just have way, way, WAY too many CS graduates now, who are competing with AI and Indians for low level entry coding jobs that AI/Indians is great at. High school students need to be advised that going into tech right now is a really bad idea. Go into the trades, traditional engineering, business, etc... anything but tech.

62139121
>>62139088
>We just have way, way, WAY too many CS graduates now,
and lawyers, they're everywhere

62139130
>>62139121
>Everybody's got a lawyer and the lawyers need to make their fees!

62139133
>>62139088
>its specifically tech
Tech is just the most recent addition to the list of fields completely killed by indians and outsourcing. Look up what entry level engineers made 30-40 years ago. In the 90s starting pay for Mechanical engineers would be around 100k now.

62139185
>>62139133
There's always sanitation engineering roles available, and they'll accept you with any degree as long as you have 2 braincels to rub together.

62139356
>>62139185
mop flip mop dip is correct

62139384
>>62138892
i got the last 4 jobs i interviewed for over the last 9 years. but im a rockstar.

62139390
>>62139185
>>62139356
The right answer is dip, dip, potato chip

62139492
>>62139185
>>62139356
>>62139390
As a former fast food mopper, the answer is
>mop
>mop
>mop
>flip
>mop
>mop
>mop
>dip

62139537
>>62139492
I didn't pay enough attention and in hindsight, I realize you are indeed true in your statement.

62139686
>>62138892
Normies and psychopaths have joined forces to engage in all-out war against the autist. They harvest the negative energy we secrete when they tell us everything is fine, and any dispossession we face is a skill issue. The name of the game is maximization of mental exhaustion within the autist population.

One person slaves in a kitchen all day and makes $80, meanwhile a foid sells butthole pics and makes $800k. There's no logic you can apply to modern society.

62139734
>>62139686
Why are so many people buying butthole pics?

62140302
>>62139686
The beginning of covid was them giving back some of their advantages and losing some of their leverage to exploit us temporarily and oh did they screech.

62141809
>>62138892
You don't understand, getting work shouldn't be easy. Doing work is supposed to be the easy part.

62141826
>>62138892
It's the same as dating, a humiliation ritual. And no one wants to admit that they're participating in a humiliation ritual. So all this cope is just a rationalisation by people who think they are "hustlers" no different to guy getting surgery to looksmax so they can get a swipe from a single mother. It's all pathetic and desperate. I suggest reading Uncle Ted.

62145635
>>62138892
>>62138969
>>62139686
>>62141826
You're all reading way too far into this, it's simply the just world fallacy in action. It's not comforting to think that your career or lack thereof is the result of arbitrary factors outside of your control. It's fucking miserable and demotivating to think that way and I don't blame normies for rejecting it.

item.title: Today, some guy said to my friend >you are lucky your parents are rich, not everyone is so lucky My friend goes >wtf doe… — 22 replies — 21.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Today, some guy said to my friend >you are lucky your parents are rich, not everyone is so lucky My friend goes >wtf doe…

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-04-23 02:38 UTC

OP

Today, some guy said to my friend
>you are lucky your parents are rich, not everyone is so lucky
My friend goes
>wtf does that even mean? How come your parents aren’t rich? Are your parents stupid? You make it seem like being rich is based on luck and not intelligence. Does that mean your parents aren’t stupid AND unlucky?
I have noticed a trend of the have-nots trying to pretend sons and daughters of rich parents “didn’t earn the money.”
Imagine thinking Barron Trump didn’t “earn his money” or Elon’s kid also.
I noticed the bottom 90% pretend it was just luckto make themselves feel better.
Like come on, even your parents aren’t not rich which means you come from bad stock.
Like okay, sure, “daddy’s money.” How come your dad is poor?
Thoughts, /biz/?

Early replies (first 3)

62144825
>>62144801
They're ngmi

62144840
>median HOUSEHOLD income
Why do they lie so much
A 22 year old in 1985 earning 20k is objectively different from a 22yo in 2023 earning 45k.

And median household income, at a time when people got married earlier is completely different from the median now where people get married later. So 22yos are actually even poorer, usually starting around 30k.

They lie so much it's no wonder people can't afford shit.

62144845
>>62144825
I swear people who say “daddy’s money” are poor. Like lets say someone is rich and has kids, would that person ever say “daddy’s money?”
It would not even make sense.
Like “mom’s basement” is another insult working class brokies use.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62144850
>>62144801
vote for open borders to add more illegals to compete with for housing and jobs. illegals obviously dont hurt the working class in any way ;)

62144853
>>62144840
What are you talking about?
The chart already shows the home price increasing even more.

62144886
>>62144840
If people couldn't afford shit, shit'd go cheaper, wouldn't it? There are buyers for these $2M SFHs. Plenty of.

62144956
>>62144886
Yeah, the masses are poor and think everyone is as poor as them. They think they are important too.

62144966
reddit board

62144976
>>62144801
Is this a purposeful fake engagement ragebait retardpost?

62145211
>>62144840
>now where people get married later
Get married earlier then, since you know it's such a massive wealth multiplier you don't really have an excuse to keep sitting in your mother's boyfriend's basement sulking about how poor you are.

62145255
>>62144801
Sounds like how nepo babies talk

62145262
>>62144801
>I have noticed a trend of the have-nots trying to pretend sons and daughters of rich parents “didn’t earn the money.”

IQ is largely genetic but also success was much more nepotism & regionally based 1-2 generations ago so yes it is just luck
But basically if you're less successful than your parents you're a loser and will never be self made

62145301
>>62145262
> But basically if you're less successful than your parents you're a loser
Brokie cope. How can Arnold’s son be more successful than Arnold?
> and will never be self made
Another brokie cope. Who cares?
>what color is your bugatti?
Exactly.

62145313
>>62144886
supply is artificially limited and there are plenty of rich people

62145449
>>62145211
Why would a MAN get married?
Girls only think about and get married once they see their looks fade. Imagine following a girls biological clock. Endless supply of girls turning 18, there is no rush.

62145481
>>62145262
every time i hear a normie complaining about literally anything i just shit about 3 solid pounds of shit out of my ass, feels a lot better afterwards

62145487
>>62144825
This counts people holding grossly overinflated homes valued at 7 figs that no one will ever buy for that much. Its a meaningless stat.

62145498
>>62145487
Yeah, it is called networth. But houses are expensive for a reason.

62145521
>>62145301
>Brokie cope. How can Arnold’s son be more successful than Arnold?
midwit cope
Plenty of people far more successful than their rich parents like pic related elon and trump who are both retards. But somehow you're content in your parent's shadow

62145525
>>62144976
yes he makes the "people dont think im retired in my 20s and theyre stupid" threads too

62145534
>>62145449
So stay poor then, just stop whining about it. It was your choice to trade pussy for money.

62145662
>>62145521
Brokie cope. And you know it. He used his parent’s money anyways which is fine with me. He just grew it.

item.title: 92000 usd is actually very cheap for a TRD Pro Toyota Tacoma. — 12 replies — 17.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
92000 usd is actually very cheap for a TRD Pro Toyota Tacoma.

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-04-23 02:59 UTC

OP

92000 usd is actually very cheap for a TRD Pro Toyota Tacoma.

Early replies (first 3)

62142701
>>62142695
>t. poorfaggoto Toyota sarariman

62142707
92k depreciating goybucks is a hilarious overpay on a turbo 4 cylinder hybrid motor that sharts itself.

62143527
>tacoma

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62143549
>>62142695
40k u can get a nice barely used lexus

62143586
>>62143549
Yeah I would rather have two used GX460s than one TaRD Pro 4 banger. Or a GX460 and an ES350. Or a GX460 and an ES300h and save on gas. That's the proper place for a 4 cylinder hybrid - cruising in a quiet luxury sedan on a road trip. Not in your spotless $90k truck with a 2 inch factory lift.

62143798
Honda Passport Trailsport is superior in terms of quality and luxury, but if you go off roading and mudding then TRD is it.

62144513
>>62142695
she fucks that horse.

62144696
>>62142695
You slickers have no use for a Tacoma.

62145432
toyota tacomas are now 4 cylinder engines, just like my ford fiesta. i want to hear the cope posts from the tacoma fanboys and what olympic medal mental gymnastics they will have to do to explain why this is a net positive.

I will wait.

62145670
>>62142695
There is no way they are selling a Tacoma for 92k

62145716
I got a 2025 SR5 with the TRD lift kit brand new for 39K. Even has 4wd. I feel like you got ripped off due.

62145729
>>62145432
3k civic bros rise the FUCK up

item.title: SpaceX IPO side investments? — 6 replies — 3.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
SpaceX IPO side investments?

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-04-23 02:05 UTC

OP

Been seeing quite a few things about the SpaceX IPO and recommendations to invest in couple side companies that will benefit from the IPO. A lot of them make sense.

Curious if it's a good line of thinking or not.

Listed companies:
DXYZ (Destiny Tech100 Inc)
RONB (Baron First Principles ETF)
BPTRX (Baron Partners Fund - Retail)
XOVR (Entrepreneur Private-Public Crossover ETF)
ASTS (AST SpaceMobile, Inc.)
ARKVX (ARK Venture Fund)
ANET (Arista Networks)
LHX (L3Harris Technologies)
SATS (EchoStar Corporation)
QQQX (Nuveen Nasdaq 100 Dynamic Overwrite Fund)

$1k to each?

Early replies (first 3)

62144752
How important oil and natural gas for space sector?

62145184
Lane Bryant because those rocket women are thicc

62145318
spacex doesnt' have "side companies"...
Buying random customers of spacex... or worse even their fucking competitors is delusional
Of course normies know nothing about space so maybe it works

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62145343
>>62144752
0%

62145363
>>62145318
Didn't say they were SpaceX's "side companies you stupid faggot.

62145552
>>62144752
its all needed to make the shit...lin was an obvious play earlier this year, but idk if i'd buy now. definitley a hold forever kind of company when you buy at the right price though

item.title: Insider info here and i'm only sharing it because i'm a greasy nigger who's made millions from biz over the decade so wh… — 45 replies — 93.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Insider info here and i'm only sharing it because i'm a greasy nigger who's made millions from biz over the decade so wh…

Open thread • Replies: 45 • Updated: 2026-04-23 03:11 UTC

OP

Insider info here and i'm only sharing it because i'm a greasy nigger who's made millions from biz over the decade so whatever
there is a CRINGE coin you ALL know coming out with some state of the art shit very soon (already in the final stages of the audit)
Not giving the name because I'm not a total fag but trust me lol you all fucking know it lmao
Just remember you were too retarded to buy it at these levels and 5k could of put you in the top 50 holders lol
that'll be 150-180k within the next couple weeks lol some of you niggers might remember or know my wallet even
if someone guesses it i'll confirm

Early replies (first 3)

62141013
Vechain? Nano?

62141015
>>62140986
>5k could of put you in the top 50 holders
i thought it was link until you said that
anon we're retarded

62141016
Someone will figure it out lol i'm not going to name drop this shit it's too cringe and it's made way too many waves of cringe on these boards over the years
i mean chainlink is close lol but not it

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62141123
i'll watch this thread until someone guesses it

62141126
someone got it

62141132
>>62141126
So fxcking bullish.

62141145
im too embarrased even when being anonomous to disclose who got it right (ashamed to even buy it because of how many times theyve fucked biz in the past lol) but theyre about to pop off
sniff it out im not shilling it directly that's for fag third worlders to do.

62141155
>>62141145
avi it is then
thanks
>>62141084
>>62141132

62141170
>>62141155

Digits leading us to the troof. I still have a bag of that dog with goggles coin from years ago

62141177
>>62141145
now this feels like ur trying to get people to buy coins they themselves think are scams

62141206
>>62141069
It can’t be avi, $5k puts you at the number 1 holder. And if he meant 5k avi that’s no where near the top. The only options are dash file Tezos or icp.

62141213
good night anons, wagmi

62141221
>>62141206
ICP is too big, 5k usd would only get you 12k icp https://www.coincarp.com/currencies/dfinity/richlist/

62141224
>>62141213

62141228
>>62141213

62141281
Bro deleted it lol

62141324
ok bro i chucked 100 of my minimum wagebucks at the doggles, thanks/fuck you in 2 weeks

62142141
awwwwwwwwwwwwww yeaeah

62142161
>>62140986
RLC baggies still exist in 2026? I thought you all killed yourselves. They should put you in a wildlife preserve bro

62142203
It’s insane clown posse just admit it

62142232
>>62141044
Up 15% in the last 24 hours lmao

62143703
stixil humiliation rape ritual continues with new year of baggies, im here for it

62143820
Check out CHIP, at 11c right now....
Up almost 2x what it was last night.

Forget this BS faggot who won't tell you a WINNER, makes you GUESS like children.

He knows nothing, stringing you alone.

Get YOUR chips in CHIP, today!

62145118
>>62140986

62145140
>>62140986
it's ethereum isn't it?
nothing is more cringe than eth nowadays

62145152
>>62140986

The currency of Tau.net

An self amending continuously automatically voting Blockchain that serves its users and becomes a sort of a living organism.

62145326
Gayviator is the summer play this year

62145472
I’ve been burned too many times by retards shilling complete trash here to buy this shit.
The larping kiltnigger cost me $3k.
The taraxatard cost me $700 before I decided the fat fuck dev is a scammer nigger and sold at a loss.
The altheatard managed to shill to enough retards to 10x the price briefly and I had the opportunity to sell my bags to other retards then it proceeded to gigadump over several days.. Thank god I sold it before that happened.
Niggers track record here is terrible.

62145500
>>62145472
Oh I forgot to add that faggot bubblemaps token shilling nigger that shilled that piece of shit at the top. Had to wait a few days to sell at a tiny 1% profit after it gigadumped because that nigger shilled after the whales bought.
I hate that nigger so much for shilling at the top.

62145545
>>62141126
Tezos it is.

62145743
aviator bridge was so good and used so much that they have to make another one
what a nigger scam peddled by grifters since 2024

62145774

item.title: Do you flex your BTC to your family or nah? — 22 replies — 60.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Do you flex your BTC to your family or nah?

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-04-23 03:18 UTC

OP

Wondering if people actually talk about their BTC with family or just keep it lowkey.

>We never really know how long we have in life, so is it better to share it or keep it private?

Early replies (first 3)

62138671
>>62138453
I only have 1 so that's nothing to flex

62138680
People with more money than you won't give a shit and people with less money than you will resent you.

Keep your mouth shut and if anyone questions your purchases or your lifestyle, pretend to be in debt.

62138700
>>62138453
Keep that shit private, openly talking about how much money you have casually is trashy. Only discuss money with family with the intention of planning / sharing, otherwise they'll always have the thought that you have more to yourself. Speaking from experience with a good family, money brings out the absolute worst in people.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62139442
>>62138671
Okay, but what if something bad happens to you? You still won’t be able to pass it on even if it’s 1 BTC, even though its value could be much higher in the future.

62139556
>>62139442
>You still won’t be able to pass it on even if it’s 1 BTC,
What are you talking about kek. You sound confused

62139618
>>62138453
i make mom clean my room for 5 satoshis

62140241
>>62138680
Okay, I understand what you're saying about.

62141418
>>62138700
You also have a point. Do you keep it for your soon to be senpai or when you're married?

62141768
>>62138700
Bad decision

62141874
>>62139618
why so poor?

62142197
>>62138671
I can't believe people on this board are shrimps

62142226
Im still in my 40s and Ive got 63 btc. I'm taking care of my health and aiming until 100 years old. I will enjoy my money for the rest of my life.

62142464
I don't tell anyone how much crypto i have, if my gf asks where I got so much money from I tell her it was from saving it, not going to target to drop $200 because I'm bored.

62142867
>>62139556
I’m not confused. I’m just saying it would be a waste if he couldn’t pass it on in case something happened to him, and his BTC would just end up stuck there.

62142904
>>62138453
I haven’t even told my imaginary friend. Been holding for over half a decade now.

62143627
>>62139618
send proof or else.

62144918
>>62142197
It's sad

62145039
>>62138453
story behind the pic?

62145144
>>62145039
There was a thread here about what you spend your gains on and someone spelt a lot on a horsehide leather jacket. People kept telling him to post the jacket.

62145154
I just lie about my personal wealth. Everyone thinks I'm poor and buys me things. If they thought I had money, they'd beg me for money. Fuck that. Plus they'd probably talk about me to bad people and I'd get bad people after me.

Stealth wealth is the way to go.

62145244
>>62139442
>>62142867
Well you tell your fucking wife or put it in your will where to find the 5 parts to your wallet (of which they need 2)

62145795
>>62141768
What do you mean?

item.title: Polkadot - Mumbai Warrior Edition — 80 replies — 9.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - Mumbai Warrior Edition

Open thread • Replies: 80 • Updated: 2026-04-22 19:32 UTC

OP

Hello my fellow PolkaCurry brothers. We are again here, for another thread where we discuss everything related to DOT, Mumbai (the city of DOT), curry and more. The last thread was a resounding success, we managed to keep it alive for literally 41 days. We got paid 11500 + 5*150 = 12250 DOT for the thread and are now living in a period of enlightenment while eating with METAL spoons. Brothers, we have truly reached enlightenment. WE, the Mumbai Warriors will strike yet again and collect generations worth of money !

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

Early replies (first 3)

62115437
We are eating with our METAL spoons in Mumbai. No more hands my brethren. We eat with out spoons, like proud warriors.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62115624
>>62115424
>>62115437

Still gonna larp as an Indian?

62115793
>>62115437

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62121953
>>62121862
>>62121922

62122759
>>62121922
>>62121953

Chart looks good

But in the last thread weren't you shorting DOT

62123242
>>62122759
Panjali, be quite you fool (https://boards.4chan.org/biz/thread/61932834#p62069282). Do you want the Mumbai warriors to eat with their hands again? Or do you want to upgrade from our metal spoons to silver and then golden spoons? We, the Mumbai warriors, are painting the graph bullish in order to lure people in, then we strike with a x125 short. We actually been holding short open since 55$. The whole of Mumbai will eat from that singular short. Panjali, you have much to learn about the Vishnu ways. It's ok my son. I forgive you. Together we are strong, divided we are weak.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62124594
>>62123242
Way to promote a project then rug people

62125019
Didint read thread, i Will let My id decide if i buy some DOT when i get home

62125037
>>62125019
w4, E3, Wasb, and purple colour... Yes.. i see...

Not buying.

62125463
this is the most demoralizing dot thread

62126538
>>62124594
>>62125019
>>62125037
>>62125463
Good job my fellow warriors of Mumbai. Thank you for keeping the thread alive with your comments.

>this is the most demoralizing dot thread
How so my Mumbai brother?

>Way to promote a project then rug people
Yes Ganesha. We play the peoples hearts and then we stab them in their hearts.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62126677
Sol 8>297 Is 37X
Dot 1,15 x37= 42

62126931
>>62126538
Holy shit. Are you actually an autistic retard?

62126975
>>62126931
Kamal, are you getting upset again because your mother took away your metal spoon and ate with it? There is plenty of metal spoons for everyone my young warrior of Mumbai.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62127244
>>62126677
Vrishank, that is really good mathematics. Education is really paying off I see. Very good. You might even become a doctor or a lawyer. Yes, you shall reach to the heavens of nirvana one day my young Vrishank. I, Vishnu Kumar, believe in you and so should you my young Vishnu warrior of the mind chain, my young Mumbai warrior, my young metal spoon owner. Soon, we will have silver spoons for everyone, then gold spoon and after that we reach the nirvana of diamond spoons. The whole of Mumbai will eat.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62127578
>>62126975
Yep. You are officially a retard.

62127673
>>62127578
Vrishank, did you just really say that with the intent you did? Is there anything wrong? Are you not feeling ok?

62128205
>>62126931
>>62127578
you are talking to a bot (and so am I)

62128210
>>62128205
BIP BOP BIP BOP


Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62129040
>>62128205
No, its actual autistic retard

62129456
>>62129040
Impossible, look at the time stamps of the posts

62129518
>>62129040
>>62129456
No, it's actually Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62131415
100$ end of bull run


Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62131658
It is still cheap right now. Don't buy my bags at 25$. You can buy them now at 1.28$. Don't FOMO in at 50$ guys.

62131731
>>62116681
>What exactly does it have that sets it apart from Solona and ETH?
Solana has a success rate associated with it's transactions (see https://solscan.io/analytics). That's kind of fucked up that there's a 10 to 20% chance your tx might not get processed, right? I've never seen a DOT tx fail. DOT doesn't even measure failed tx https://data.parity.io/parachain-stats?selection-mode=include&frequency=daily&relay-chain=polkadot&chains= because there are none. If you submit a tx it'll complete within like 6s to 30s. The longest a tx takes is when you transfer from one corechain to another. So when you bridge (use a HRMP channel) between corechains. And even that never took longer than like 40s from my personal experience. It's bizarre to me that literally up to 20% of the tx simply fail.
Another thing is that SOL literally has more "vote" transactions than anything else; https://solscan.io/analytics#txs . And vote transactions are LITERALLY validator messages to keep the network running. DOT doesn't count that as a tx at all. 60 to 80% of the tx on SOL are from validators to keep the network running. That's insane to think about that you need more tx to run the network than the users use to execute stuff. People are stupid for thinking that SOL processes 3k TPS. More than half of that is just to keep the network running. It's a literal scam number to be honest. It's just to make the normies believe that the network isn't a scam. The number of transactions is completely fake in a sense.
And again, the funny thing is that 1 core of DOT will be able to handle more TPS than the whole of SOL once either JAM or NOMT hit. Maybe even before that once the 500ms Basti blocks hit. If you combine 500ms blocks + JAM + NOMT one core of DOT will be able to probably do anywhere between 2 to 5x more than SOL as a whole and DOT has LITERALLY 100 cores and this will also probably be scaled to more sooner or later. It'll sooner or later scale to about 300 to 500 cores.

62131777
>>62116681
>>62131731
The 300-500 cores are after the network upgrades to 1000 validators from 600 and after a few more future optimizations. But DOT can then scale further by adding more relay chains. So, currently there is 1 relay chain that connects all the corechains (while they also have direct connections through the HRMP channels) and this relay chain has 600 active validators at any given time. At some point DOT could scale further by having multiple relay chains and separate validators on each relay chain and then having a "relay chain" that connects the relay chains. Sure, this would fragment liquidity in a sense but you could still transfer assets between the relay chains in probably like 30s or so... So in a sense the fragmented liquidity would be a non-problem. DOT is truly scalable while other chains simply are not. DOT covered all the problems because Gavin used to develop ETH and noticed "OMG, this won't work out" and then he left ETH in order to develop DOT. DOT is ETH done right in a sense. ETH is just BTC with smart contracts and DOT is an actual solution to all the problems that plague ETH and all the other smart contract platforms.

62135297
100$ end of bull run

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62137662
Soon we shall reach the nirvana of 75$ my Mumbai brothers. Then we shall eat with silver spoons.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62139771
You are not the body, you are not the mind, you are not your emotions. You are the silent DOT in the infinity of consciousness. You are just a wave in the ocean of consciousness that forgot it is part of the ocean. For when rememberance dawns, the mystery is not solved. It is lived.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62141372
My brethren, the time is nigh. We're break out. The wave of consciousness is breaking free from the ocean floor.

62143550
Any second now BTC will erupt into a storm that has never been seen before. DOT will soon start it's ascension to the heavens for 75$+. We, the Mumbai warriors, shall eat with silver spoons very soon. The time is coming my warriors. You still have to to buy a big fat bag of DOT before you get priced out.

62143825
>>62129518
>>62131415
>>62131658
>>62131731
>>62131777
>>62135297
>>62139771
>>62143550

This is sad. Also stop larping as an Indian and perhaps you'll get engagement

62144010
>>62143825
>This is sad.
My brother, the sadness is in your own heart. Not mine. You are destroying yourself.

>Also stop larping as an Indian and perhaps you'll get engagement
My brother, I am the one and only Vishnu Kumar. Born and raised in India. I am brown as the mud itself. There is no denying it. I am a true Mumbai warrior. I am a proud Indian.
Also Panjali, didn't people teach you that questions are fundamental for a healthy conversation. My young Panjali, I am not here for engagement. If you asked I would've told you so. Oh young Panjali. You still have a long way ahead of you. If accept, I let go, I move on and I forgive you young Panjali.

Thank you for you kind bump of the thread Panjali. I shall later come to your house and give you your share of the profits.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

item.title: Arbitrum revealed itself to be a CEX in chain form — 27 replies — 6.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Arbitrum revealed itself to be a CEX in chain form

Open thread • Replies: 27 • Updated: 2026-04-22 23:22 UTC

OP

https://x.com/arbitrum/status/2046435443680346189?s=20

They had the power all along to freeze any address's funds and take their crypto. Can't even cope with "not your keys, not your crypto" here.

Early replies (first 3)

62138208
>>62138188
My crypto is on ETH, so its my keys & my crypto

62138253
>>62138188
All L2s are centralized, this isnt news.

62138290
>>62138253
This is a different story in that this L2 has the power to spoof your private key. That's NEVER in the discussion of discussing if an L2 is centralized. When L2 and centralization is discussed, it revolves around the ordering of transactions and who controls the nodes.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62138320
>>62138290
Well youre gay? So yah.

62138408
>>62138188
Every l2 is a centralized scam

62138910
>>62138290
It's not the private keys they control it's the tokens.

62138917
>>62138290
>to spoof your private key
no lol. this is very different

62138931
>>62138910
>>62138917

So what is it then if I run a chain and can take possession of your ETH or whatever coin in your wallet? I know USDC can blacklist wallets, is it this at protocol level?

62138967
>>62138931
>is it this at protocol level?
Yes, they can move ETH since it is the chain's native token. Moving ERC20s would be more difficult. What they can't do is impersonate the address, i.e. they cannot forge signatures and pretend to be the private key owner

62139424
>>62138967
What else are they capable of doing that people should be aware of if the security council comes together to conspire against them? Being able to restrict your wallet and deprive you of your own ETH is a really really big deal.

62139448
>>62139424
No it isn't. it's been known for centuries that L2s were always centralized and now that skellyman denounced them there's no incentive for otherwise

62139494
>>62139448
The discussions of centralization were around node transaction handling. Not being able to control the the very ETH in your wallet. You are conflating things.

62139604
>>62138188
This is why I hold LUNC
the only truly decentralized chain

62139909
>>62138188
> executed a technical approach to move funds
kek, L2s are so worthless. ethereum should have never gone into that direction so heavily. it was obvious that the devs would never give up power within their own rollup.

62140324
anything kikes do is a scam

62140341
>>62140324
Let the goyim deal in the real world

62140513
>>62139424
They can do things that a typical validator set can do. This is like arguing that mining pools can conspire to fork the chain. Can they? Sure. Is there an incentive to do it? Not really.

62140566
>>62140513
>They can do things that a typical validator set can do.

And I want to know what that entails. If some entity mindhacked all the nodes in the BTC network or in the ETH network, they still can't take your crypto. All they can do is censor your transactions. To insert fake transactions and rewrite history, they'd have to go through the network to rewrite each node's history individually to recreate the blockchain. What Arbitrum did is out of the scope of what you'd expect from a validator set. So what can they do?

62140574
>>62140566
>they still can't take your crypto.
That's not correct. Blockchains are not immutable, they are democratic, and anything can change at the protocol level simply by changing the node software. There is an active proposal right now to censor Satoshi's coins, and if/when bitcoin decides to fork into quantum resistance, those coins will be de facto censored.

62140675
>>62138408
Eth is a centralized scam
EVM is a dogshit idea that only got traction because of its ability to be gatekept, controlled, and manipulated.

62141600
>>62140574
yeah but this is not what we're looking at here. they didn't fork the smart contract or anything like that. instead there seems to be a mechanism in place right now that allows the arbitrum conceal of gigafags to move user funds to another address.

62141625
I remember the chainlink schizos and how they believed arbitrum was also chainlink lmfao

62141991
>>62140574
>That's not correct. Blockchains are not immutable, they are democratic, and anything can change at the protocol level simply by changing the node software. There is an active proposal right now to censor Satoshi's coins, and if/when bitcoin decides to fork into quantum resistance, those coins will be de facto censored.

And all the nodes have to consciously upgrade to this new software version. At the very least there's still a democratic consensus at node operator level to accept such a proposal. That's still an acceptable level of decentralization.

>>62141600
>instead there seems to be a mechanism in place right now that allows the arbitrum conceal of gigafags to move user funds to another address.
Exactly. The only people who have access to this feature is the security council who WE have zero control or influence over. These guys are too weak to sing "Niggas in Paris" in front of their own mirror without breaking down due to guilt over patriarchal oppression and the lamentations of the oppressed.

(They're not on the council, but this is example of the folks in the industry)
https://x.com/PatrickAlphaC/status/2012634410764398795?s=20
https://x.com/va_joe/status/2042239851387290022?s=20

Are we to honestly believe that if the FBI comes slamming on the door, they'll be "decentralized" enough to reject an unjust order to move X user's funds?

What do in

62142780
>>62138188
This. BUMP

62142948
>>62141991
>And all the nodes have to consciously upgrade to this new software version. At the very least there's still a democratic consensus at node operator level to accept such a proposal.
Kind of. In practice, only the main hashrate contributors matter, and that is a vanishingly small group. The classic hardline thinking about decentralization just doesn't comport with reality for any blockchain anymore, and likely never will again.

62143434
>>62138290
Are you retarded?

62145045
Man I wish I sold my eth a few months ago.

item.title: /sifm/ - structure and interpretation of financial markets — 7 replies — 5.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
/sifm/ - structure and interpretation of financial markets

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-04-23 02:24 UTC

OP

https://docs.hypercall.xyz/insights/jpm-collar-q1-2026/


prev >>62136320

still working on this. GEX is pretty lazy heuristic if you know the JPM collar at least you know JPMs GEX on that position. I'm not sure how often SPX lands within the collar.

- https://seekingalpha.com/article/4592165-what-you-need-to-know-about-jheqx-collars-and-the-impact-on-spy
- https://spotgamma.com/jpm-morgan-collar-trade/
- https://am.jpmorgan.com/us/en/asset-management/adv/products/jpmorgan-hedged-equity-fund-i-46637k281


I'm just spitballing, the collar is just a hedge in JPMs equity portfolio, so it's not like they're gonna force SPX to stay within the collars range. if SPX lands above the collar for example, it means their equity portfolio is doing well.

Early replies (first 3)

62144585
Looking for more stuff like the JPM collar where you can actually infer dealer gamma without making assumptions, here's some Claude slop on that. This should let you work out at least some signed gamma exposure without making any assumptions.

And I think it's the COUNTERPARTY to the JPM collar that would be hedging, not JPM. The collar is the hedge for JPM.

dealer gamma / gex is just one small fry in the overall picture here I'm just trying to see what % of OI on the options chain you can actually know anything about without making the assumptions in the standard GEX model (dealers net selling calls, net buying puts).

62144657
I think the best ideas from the last thread btw were here
>>62138928
>>62138945

trying to map out the options volume flow and whatnot first to see what % of OI I can apply signage too with reasonable confidence, then exploring the viability of fingerprinting of order flows

62144979
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2D4oIraa9qM

this is the best explanation so far of the price action from EOM March to beginning of April, I think this person nailed the mechanical drivers.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62145055
>>62144547
I just came here to complain that I missed out on that massive pump when Trump got elected in 2024, and the biden pump of 2021, and have wanted to kill myself most days since then.

62145083
>>62145055
The Trump pump surprised me too, I thought we'd dump or crab at least a little bit on the expectation of increased volatility plus tariffs. You can imagine my frustration when we finally dumped on tariffs and the market acted like this was some sort of surprise even though that was his whole platform. But I guess the market knows he's a pumper and cares about being near ATH all the time.

I'm trying to completely abandon trading based off of 'what I think should happen' and instead only trade based on what the market is signaling it wants to do, and trade in that implied direction or with the volatility trend. I think a lot of permabears have trouble with that. You're just pissing into the wind otherwise.

62145176
>>62145055
I'm also understanding more lately that the US markets are an arm of the 'global financial USDgang terrorist regime' and when framed in that manner things make a lot more sense. I didn't know until today that Bessent is harbinger of financial apocalypse for countries agains the USD
https://www.nytimes.com/2024/11/26/business/scott-bessent-wall-street-treasury.html

62145615
i think this was the best idea from last thread
>>62139242
but i wish i had something for you. anytime any particular set of observations seems to be the trendsetter, there's always some other piece. it's just as much an art as it is analysis. if there's few to follow then you may just be on the right path. you add alot to the board anon
>>62144979
didn't watch but saw the title, the tape has screamed dispersion traders leveraged to the tits for weeeeks now to me. seems worse than into liberation day last year. floor on implied index vol until an unwind would be the assumption

item.title: XRP to $800 incoming You have been warned. — 22 replies — 5.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
XRP to $800 incoming You have been warned.

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-04-22 23:30 UTC

OP

XRP to $800 incoming

You have been warned.

Early replies (first 3)

62141456
This would give me literally everything I want and that's the main reason it won't happen.
Not the powers that be, my female friends, nor the fates themselves want me to have these things.

62141457
>>62141454
Stay in your cuck containment thread, faggot
Herbs

62141458
>>62141456
Don't black pill.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62141464
>>62141456
If you already hold XRP this means you already have been chosen. It's only a matter of time my friend.

62141467
>>62141458
I hope, but I won't allow myself to believe. And allow me to have this power at your own peril.

62141476
>>62141467
NEVER lose hope. We will achieve our wildest dreams.

62141554
How does 11 cents sound?

62141569
>>62141454
Interesting. I'd heard 589 some months ago. If it does what it sets out to then yeah even 1K seems possible in time. Pops has a bag. I'm thinking I'll be rotating gains into it since I can't put a significant amount into it right now.

62141576
>>62141554
sounds good. then I can load up my bags.

62141577
>>62141554
Like a good time to buy more

>>62141569
You did the right thing sir, please kindly invest all your money in the XRP as possible

62141906
dare i say...SO BULLISH XRP
BOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62142054
>>62141454
>>62141456
>>62141457
>>62141458
>>62141464
>>62141467
>>62141476
>>62141554
I'm gonna..... I'm gonna...
IM GONNA FXCKING BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62142257
>>62141454
You'll be lucky to ever see $3 in this life you retarded kike

62142285
before or after they dump the remaining 40 billion tokens?

62142772
>>62141457
Cope

62143167
>>62141464
>>62141456
>>62141454
ask any middle aged poor person in crypto and they hold xrp thinking it's gonna make them a millionaire

62143946
KABOOOOOM

62143997
>>62142257
If I hypothetically have a friend who owns this coin and already lost money on it but holds it just to dump it after it's final pump, is it better to just cut my losses now and spend it elsewhere?

62144327
>>62143167
What about middle aged upper class people?
My brother is a millionaire (from career and stonks) and he's the biggest xrp enioyer I've met yet

62144503
>>62141454
herpie xerpies can't stay hard
thankfully they make a pill for that

62144536
>>62143167
For me and my conservative estimates, I only think it can help do so if stuff I'm already in takes off first and then I rotate into XRP. We'll see.

62145072
>>62143167
seeing how boomers have been absolutely right with the stock market and homes... they might be onto something

62142149

62142263
My i just had a big scare with herp recently. Turns out it was just my vitiligo medication. I thought it was this nasty bitch I rawed.

62142929
>>62142149
crazy, didn't know that. same like USA.

Here are the top 25 countries by estimated number of people living with HIV (PLHIV), with adult prevalence (%) for ages 15–49 where available. Figures are rounded estimates from UNAIDS/WHO and other global data sources (most recent published year 2023–2025).

South Africa — ~9,200,000; 17.3%
India — ~2,350,000; 0.2%
Nigeria — ~2,450,000; 1.3%
Mozambique — ~2,480,000; 12.6%
Kenya — ~1,600,000; 4.0%
Uganda — ~1,400,000; 3.0%
Tanzania — ~1,700,000; 3.9%
Zimbabwe — ~2,460,000; 23.1%
Malawi — ~1,640,000; 11.4%
Ethiopia — ~630,000; 0.9%
Zambia — ~1,200,000; 11.0%
Brazil — ~870,000; 0.6%
United States — ~1,200,000; 0.4%
Russia — ~1,200,000; 0.4%
Pakistan — ~210,000; 0.1%
Democratic Republic of the Congo — ~520,000; 0.8%
Indonesia — ~540,000; 0.2%
Cameroon — ~560,000; 3.6%
Afghanistan — ~50,000; 0.1%
Uganda (if duplication) — note: regional lists vary; see notes below.
Sudan — ~210,000; 0.4%
Vietnam — ~250,000; 0.3%
Argentina — ~140,000; 0.4%
Côte d’Ivoire — ~360,000; 2.9%
Ghana — ~330,000; 1.7%

item.title: bitcoin bros are so fucking retarded, their entire investment thesis is "its the digital era bro", and their shield is a… — 7 replies — 4.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
bitcoin bros are so fucking retarded, their entire investment thesis is "its the digital era bro", and their shield is a…

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-04-23 02:05 UTC

OP

bitcoin bros are so fucking retarded, their entire investment thesis is "its the digital era bro", and their shield is always "you missed the 2020 bottom, now cope"

you fucking retards the whole thing was basically rigged from the start, the main driver for bitcoin is actually FOMO and dumbass populist governments that try to appeal to the tech savvy onions echelon that learned what BTC was in 2022

yes, im a BTC permabear, and the only kind of BTC holder i respect are the neckbearded niggers that have been holding it since the silk road era

Early replies (first 3)

62145139
>>62145124
Wtf I remember normal Smosh but now there's a ghetto channel

62145150
>>62145124
Each year you deny clown world but things are getting more and more crazy. Bitcoin at 1 million dollars is the clown world magnum opus, 5 star resorts will be built in gaza, trannies and blacks will take over world governments and whites will slowly go extinct. Stop fighting it there is nothing you can do about it

62145159
>>62145124
>investment thesis
currency isn't an investment you retarded mongrel

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62145266
>>62145124

You're an idiot. My investment thesis is that Bitcoin replaces banks and makes you the sole controller of your personal wealth, even if the number doesn't go up.

cope harder.

62145293
respect.

62145333
>>62145124
My whole LIFE was rigged from the start motherfucker, that isn't stopping me. To the moon.

62145551
>>62145124
;_; got in in 2014 so I get no respect from you

I guess I'll just have to cry while living off my mere millions on the beach surrounded by supermodels ;_;
